projects.title: Rewilding the Ground
projects.description:
Rewilding the Ground
2023
Location: Vienna 
Size: Urban scale
Competition entry: Europan 17 
In collaboration with Panagis Marketos
Design team: Lida Badafareh, Panagis Marketos, Mehrshad Atashi
​
Rewilding the Ground envisions a densely populated settlement for 11,000 inhabitants, where the urban artifact and nature exist in a dynamic dialogue.

 A significant portion of the ground is preserved and dedicated to nature—an approach that contrasts with contemporary urban developments primarily driven by land value extraction. The project introduces a spectrum of habitats and nature—ranging from highly domesticated to wild—fostering diverse ecosystems and narratives of coexistence. The settlement consists

 of follies, a courtyard building, and a set of housing on pilots, all embedded within a gradient of nature of varying qualities, conditioning it and being conditioned by it. Beyond its care for nature and the ground, the project offers the city of Vienna a new attraction with diverse events and common spaces. The spatial configuration

 strategically limits car traffic while promoting mild mobility, ensuring a pedestrian-friendly landscape.

projects.title: Silent Witness – Traveler
projects.description:
Silent Witness – Traveler 

2023

Location: Crete

Competition entry: Art Prize 2024 G. & A. Mamidakis Foundation

An art installation In collaboration with Panagis Marketos

Design team: Lida Badafareh, Panagis Marketos, Mehrshad Atashi

​

Through a temporary intervention, our design unfolds a narrative of hidden nature, transforming the hotel corridor, into a theatrical stage where travelers become performers in an ongoing narrative, wandering in this enigmatic landscape for leisure and curiosity. The Travelers staying in the rooms along this corridor find themselves stepping into a passage. On one side, a door leads back to her room; on the other, the wall reveals a scene evoking familiar moments in nature—the transition from dusk to dawn, the quiet serenity of light filtering through trees. Scattered throughout the space, peep boxes rest on stones, drawing travelers' attention. Curious, they peer inside and are immediately captivated by the world revealed to them.

projects.title: Plus Minus Wall
projects.description:
Plus Minus Wall
2020-2022
Location: Damavand, Tehran 
Size: 160 m²
Status: Commissioned project- Unbuilt
Collaborators:
Local Project Supervisor: JII9 (Shirin Hadadian, Milad Salehivasegh)
Structural: Mohammad Panahi
Mechanical and Electrical: Moein Karimi
Detail designer: Behdad Shahvari, Mansour Naghdi & Afsaneh Esfandiari nia
General contractor: Raahrodesign (Amir Mokaber, Saman Haji Saffar)
​
Plus Minus Wall  offers different modes of dwelling, allowing each to express itself as an individual entity while simultaneously functioning as a cohesive whole, a house. The project consists of a garden, a corridor space, a wall, a chimney, and three single-family houses. They are aligned on a diagonal axis marked by the wall on the ground, which creates a conflict between the frontality of the land and the building’s diagonal juxtaposition. The wall demarcates the space and defines a series of related antagonisms: inside and outside, closed and open, mass and void, in-between and besides, behind and front. Apart from defining the space, the wall embeds, hides, separates, and reveals the other parts of the building.

projects.title: Nikzi Bookshop
projects.description:
Nikzi Bookshop
2019
Location: Tehran
Size: 44.3 m²
Status: Proposal
​
Within the Nikzi Bookshop’s existing space, we proposed a new interior to create an unexpected spatial continuity and discontinuity. The shop’s sequential space is disconnected by walls but integrated through colors, textures, doors, and windows. The center of the shop is distracted by twin semi-circles, which are dislocated in opposition. There are two corridors in the space, connecting the building’s interior with its exterior, one with doors and the other with windows. On the book shop’s exterior, the figuration of the interior space cuts through the existing façade of the building, flaunting its contrast to the façade’s planarity. Strollers see a ramp with handrails welcoming them towards the building from the street, but they do not see any door to enter. The entrance of the book shop is hidden, attracting the curiosity of visitors.

projects.title: This is That
projects.description:
This is that
2021
POTENZIALE 3 Doctoral Exhibition
@aut. architektur und tirol
This plan is that elevation.
This elevation is that elevation.
This frontal view is that top view.
This silhouette is that silhouette, is this silhouette, is that silhouette, and is this silhouette.
This is not that; they are alike but not identical.

projects.title: Misfit Qualities
projects.description:
Misfit Qualities 
On John Hejduk's Wall House 2
2020
The IARC Show 
@Junge Talstation, Innsbruck
Misfit Qualities attempts to challenge the tension between the real and its representation. This was carried out through studying John Hejduk’s Wall House II drawing. In the original drawing, he introduces the object and a possible way of reading the real using colors, chroma, saturation, and an oblique mode of representation. We kept the silhouettes the same but destabilized our understanding of the real through manipulation of representational qualities: colors, cast shades, shadows, absence of shadows, reflection, figuration, transparency, and texture. However, while the figuration remained the same, the new representational object blurred the distinction of the figures, which intensified the conflict between the real and its representation.

projects.title: Vase, Oval and Triangle
projects.description:
Vase, Oval and Triangle
2018
Video Animation
​
Within the MMK Museum of Modern Art by Hans Hollein, the building loosely has a triangular shape, as if someone materialized the visual cone of perspective. This perspectival point became the main attribute that has been adapted by different parts of the building, the point to which things either converge or around which they are arranged. This focal characteristic appears as the explicit quality of circular, oval, and triangular shapes in the MMK building. Here, we can see that geometry is a dominant force in transforming other entities and combining them. In this project, we are exploring how readymade objects, silhouettes of its context, geometrical figures, and the elements of architecture influence each other.

projects.title: Micromega as Discrete Interpretations
projects.description:
Micromega as Discrete Interpretations (Science City) 
2017
​
Micromega as discrete interpretations proposes an understanding of the spatial dimensions of the Micromega series of drawings by Daniel Libeskind to identify the projective and pictorial aspects of the drawings and understand their operative potentials. This project was never meant to study Micromegas based on what Libeskind claimed about it but rather to explore the comparisons and differences that have been made about the series. The operative potentials of Micromegas, such as different Cartesian coordinate systems, shallow pictorial depth, formal fragmentation, multiplied layering, indexicality, etc., reference a chronological past. Also, overlapping these operative potentials creates tension between a series of antagonisms – foreground and background, surface and solid, open and closed, behind and front, shallow and deep, scaled and scaleless, continuous and discrete, among several others.
